narrative:
DRIVER
USD leads D1 CSM at 100 in a Risk-Off High regime, driven by Fed hike repricing on rising US 10Y yields at 4.661% and safe-haven geopolitical demand pushing DXY to 99.32. USDCAD is the cleanest expression with D1 Buy signal, CAD accelerating H4 divergence flagged as critical, and Canadian inflation miss compounding CAD weakness at CSM 0; AUDUSD confirms via AUDUSD-AUDJPY 0.94 correlation cluster with AUD at D1 CSM 6. GBP H4 CSM at 100 versus D1 at 59 is the most important short-term signal — GBPUSD H4 momentum 70 rising sharply diverges from D1 sell, meaning intraday longs on GBPUSD are live but structurally fade any rally.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: GBPJPY CMP 55, H4 75 accelerating; NZDJPY CMP 57, both TFs rising
Fading: None

WATCH
BoJ intervention rhetoric escalates on USDJPY above recent highs, triggering sharp USDJPY reversal and unwinding correlated AUDJPY longs given 0.94 AUDUSD-AUDJPY cluster
Canadian CPI revision or follow-up data hits USDCAD, confirming D1 Buy extension toward next resistance
WTI Oil extending the 4.3% drop pressures CADJPY further given CAD CSM at zero and CADJPY D1 momentum only at 34

themes.theme: US Dollar strength from higher yields and Fed hike expectations
Safe-haven demand supporting USD amid geopolitical tensions
Canadian Dollar weakness from inflation miss versus expectations
Japanese Yen intervention risk limiting downside moves
Australian Dollar pressure from oil prices and risk-off sentiment
British Pound supported by fiscal relief but capped by softer labor data
Swiss Franc weaker despite strong growth as US yields dominate
Indian Rupee under pressure from elevated oil prices
Geopolitical risk premium from Iran tensions and Hormuz concerns
Precious metals selling pressure from firm USD and higher yields

key_observation: USD dominates as safe-haven with support from higher yields, Fed hike expectations, and geopolitical risk premium. Iran tensions provide ongoing support. AUD and CAD under pressure. BoE and RBA remain cautious on further hikes. Bessent signals desire to limit excessive FX volatility but USD momentum strong.
watch: Iran-US conflict escalation trajectory. BoC and RBA policy divergence. Trump administration FX commentary. Oil price direction via Gulf tensions. Canadian labor data for BoC guidance. UK inflation data for BoE.
